scope_note: QA Loxodrome Cert family [166]: certifies that loxodromes (rhumb lines = constant-bearing paths) arise naturally from QA T-operator iteration on a mod-m lattice. A discrete loxodrome = T-operator path with fixed initial direction; period = π(m) (Pisano period). Bearing spread = e²/G for direction (d,e). Mercator identity: s_φ = tanh²(ψ) where ψ = isometric latitude. Three orbit types partition loxodromes: cosmos (period 24, full circumnavigation), satellite (period 8, reduced), singularity (period 1, fixed). Theorem NT compliant. Tier 2 structural.
